title: Graph Memory Transformer (GMT)
new_code:
class FeedForward(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, config: MiniMindConfig, intermediate_size: int = None):
        super().__init__()
        self.hidden_size = config.hidden_size
        self.num_centroids = 128
        # Learned centroid bank
        self.centroids = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(self.num_centroids, config.hidden_size) * 0.02)
        # Learned directed transition matrix between centroids
        self.transition = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(self.num_centroids, self.num_centroids) * 0.02)
        # Gravitational source routing: project token to source scores
        self.source_proj = nn.Linear(config.hidden_size, self.num_centroids, bias=False)
        # Token-conditioned target selection
        self.target_proj = nn.Linear(config.hidden_size, self.num_centroids, bias=False)
        # Gated displacement readout: gate and output projection
        self.gate_proj = nn.Linear(config.hidden_size, config.hidden_size, bias=False)
        self.out_proj = nn.Linear(config.hidden_size, config.hidden_size, bias=False)
        self.act_fn = ACT2FN[config.hidden_act]

    def forward(self, x):
        # x: (batch, seq_len, hidden_size)
        # Source routing: softmax over centroids weighted by inverse distance (gravitational)
        src_scores = F.softmax(self.source_proj(x), dim=-1)  # (B, T, C)
        # Estimated source memory state: weighted sum of centroids
        src_state = src_scores @ self.centroids  # (B, T, hidden_size)
        # Transition: propagate source scores through transition matrix to get target distribution
        trans = F.softmax(self.transition, dim=-1)  # (C, C)
        trans_scores = src_scores @ trans  # (B, T, C)
        # Token-conditioned target selection: combine transition and token-conditioned target
        tgt_scores_token = F.softmax(self.target_proj(x), dim=-1)  # (B, T, C)
        tgt_scores = 0.5 * trans_scores + 0.5 * tgt_scores_token  # (B, T, C)
        # Target memory state: weighted sum of centroids
        tgt_state = tgt_scores @ self.centroids  # (B, T, hidden_size)
        # Displacement: movement from source toward target
        displacement = tgt_state - src_state  # (B, T, hidden_size)
        # Gated readout
        gate = torch.sigmoid(self.gate_proj(x))
        return self.out_proj(gate * displacement)
interpretation: The GMT paper's contribution relies on using graph-structured memory to capture relational dependencies between tokens/concepts, which requires the model to have sufficient capacity and architectural support to leverage that structured information effectively. MiniMind, being a small-scale architecture, likely lacks the representational depth to benefit from graph memory mechanisms, and the added complexity of graph-based memory retrieval instead introduces noise or optimization difficulties that hurt perplexity rather than helping it.
